fields.label: What they sell
fields.description: In 2-3 lines: (a) what they sell — products or services, (b) who buys it — type of customer, (c) how it reaches the customer — key steps in plain words.

fields.label: Engagement scope (what we're documenting)
fields.description: Pick what you want to achieve and which departments to cover. The AI will tailor every question to these choices.

fields.label: Engagement goals
fields.description: What outcome do we want? Pick one or more — the AI scopes its questions to these goals.

fields.label: Modules in scope (departments)
fields.description: How to pick: match modules to the client's actual pain. Cost reduction → usually 05-Purchase + 06-Production + 07-Inventory + 08-Logistics. ERP rollout → all relevant. Process rewrite → only the departments the client named. Single-module engagements are valid; more can be added later and merged. Module scope (1-liners): 01-Sales (channels, pricing, discounts) · 02-Product (catalog, BOMs, costing) · 03-CRM (leads, pipeline, follow-ups) · 04-O2C (quote → order → invoice → collection) · 05-Purchase (vendor selection, RFQ, PO, GRN, bill booking) · 06-Production (work orders, shop-floor tracking, QC) · 07-Inventory (RM/WIP/FG, stock counts, reorder, transfers) · 08-Logistics (dispatch, transport, warehousing, e-way) · 09-HR-Payroll (attendance, payroll, statutory) · 10-Finance (GL, banking, MIS, tax) · 11-Business-Ops (admin, IT, facility) · 12-Compliance (GST, ROC, labour, tax) · 13-Tech-Stack (software inventory, integrations) · 14-Controls (approvals, DOA, segregation of duties).

fields.label: Client's current systems
fields.description: What does the client use today? Helps AI tailor substrate-aware questions (paper / WhatsApp / Tally / ERP).

fields.label: Digital maturity notes (free text)
fields.description: Describe what runs on which system. Walk through: (a) what's in Tally / ERP — just accounting or full ops? (b) what's on paper / registers / whiteboards, (c) what's in Excel or Google Sheets, (d) what's on WhatsApp or phone calls, (e) any single-person dependency. Example: 'Tally Prime for accounts only · production tracking on shop-floor whiteboard · job cards on paper · GRN in security register · WhatsApp groups for vendor coordination · no MES.'

fields.label: Parent / earlier engagement
fields.description: If this engagement extends an earlier one (e.g. adding more departments later), link it here.

fields.label: Discovery rounds
fields.description: Each round = one meeting. Use the AI menu → Paste Transcript to drop in meeting notes; AI auto-fills answers + captures impromptu Q&A.

fields.label: Per-round review
fields.description: One tab per round. Each tab shows: original questionnaire → impromptu Q&A → extra observations (volunteered facts) → raw meeting transcript.

fields.label: Process completeness (end-to-end check)
fields.description: After each Score Coverage run, this panel shows how complete every in-scope business flow is. The engagement is ready to compile only when every flow is at ≥90%.

fields.label: Consultant feedback (your edits + lessons)
fields.description: After Compile, add YOUR additions / corrections / overrides here. Click 'Re-compile with my edits' to merge them into a new SOP draft. Click 'Save lessons to memory' to teach the AI for future engagements.

fields.label: Consultant edits & additions to the SOP
fields.description: Markdown notes describing additions, corrections, or sections to rewrite. Free-form. Re-compile reads this and merges intelligently with the AI draft.

fields.label: Lessons for memory (one per line)
fields.description: Each non-empty line becomes a permanent rule the AI loads on every future engagement. Examples: 'For Tier-2 mfg, always ask if key-man advances are being treated as Sec 17(2)(iv) perquisite.' / 'Push for actual MTC vernier check before GRN, not just the cert.' Click 'Save lessons to memory' to commit — the field clears after a successful save.

fields.label: Background job state
fields.description: Live status fields used by the JS poller for the long-running Score Coverage / Compile SOP / Reflect operations. Safe to ignore unless debugging a stuck job.
